channel.title: TensorFeed.ai - Research
channel.description: AI research papers, breakthroughs, and academic findings.

item.title: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?
item.description: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A couple of days earlier, Amazon had announced that...

item.title: The Pentagon’s culture war tactic against Anthropic has backfired
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Last Thursday, a California judge temporarily blocked the Pentagon from labeling Anthropic a supply chain risk...

item.title: The Download: brainless human clones and the first uterus kept alive outside a body
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones After operating in secrecy for years, R3 Bio, a...

item.title: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient monkey “organ sacks” as an alternative to animal...

item.title: BeSafe-Bench: Unveiling Behavioral Safety Risks of Situated Agents in Functional Environments
item.description: arXiv:2603.25747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid evolution of Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) has enabled agents to perform complex digital and physical tasks, yet their deployment as autonomous decision-makers introduces substantial...

item.title: AutoB2G: A Large Language Model-Driven Agentic Framework For Automated Building-Grid Co-Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2603.26005v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The growing availability of building operational data motivates the use of reinforcement learning (RL), which can learn control policies directly from data and cope with the complexity and uncertainty...

item.title: Semi-Automated Knowledge Engineering and Process Mapping for Total Airport Management
item.description: arXiv:2603.26076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Documentation of airport operations is inherently complex due to extensive technical terminology, rigorous regulations, proprietary regional information, and fragmented communication across multiple...

item.title: GUIDE: Resolving Domain Bias in GUI Agents through Real-Time Web Video Retrieval and Plug-and-Play Annotation
item.description: arXiv:2603.26266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large vision-language models have endowed GUI agents with strong general capabilities for interface understanding and interaction. However, due to insufficient exposure to domain-specific software...

item.title: AIRA_2: Overcoming Bottlenecks in AI Research Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.26499v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing research has identified three structural performance bottlenecks in AI research agents: (1) synchronous single-GPU execution constrains sample throughput, limiting the benefit of search; (2) a...

item.title: CADSmith: Multi-Agent CAD Generation with Programmatic Geometric Validation
item.description: arXiv:2603.26512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing methods for text-to-CAD generation either operate in a single pass with no geometric verification or rely on lossy visual feedback that cannot resolve dimensional errors. We present CADSmith,...

item.title: Stabilizing Rubric Integration Training via Decoupled Advantage Normalization
item.description: arXiv:2603.26535v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose Process-Aware Policy Optimization (PAPO), a method that integrates process-level evaluation into Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) through decoupled advantage normalization, to...

item.title: DesignWeaver: Dimensional Scaffolding for Text-to-Image Product Design
item.description: arXiv:2502.09867v2 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Generative AI has enabled novice designers to quickly create professional-looking visual representations for product concepts. However, novices have limited domain knowledge that could constrain...

item.title: A Lightweight, Transferable, and Self-Adaptive Framework for Intelligent DC Arc-Fault Detection in Photovoltaic Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.25749v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Arc-fault circuit interrupters (AFCIs) are essential for mitigating fire hazards in residential photovoltaic (PV) systems, yet achieving reliable DC arc-fault detection under real-world conditions...

item.title: Sommelier: Scalable Open Multi-turn Audio Pre-processing for Full-duplex Speech Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.25750v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: As the paradigm of AI shifts from text-based LLMs to Speech Language Models (SLMs), there is a growing demand for full-duplex systems capable of real-time, natural human-computer interaction....

item.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
item.description: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a restaurant kitchen. It is covered in flexible plastic...

item.title: The Download: the internet’s best weather app, and why people freeze their brains
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app The best snow-forecasting app for skiers isn’t a...

item.title: Here’s why some people choose cryonics to store their bodies and brains after death
item.description: This week I reported on some rather unusual research that focuses on the brain of L. Stephen Coles. Coles was a gerontologist who died from pancreatic cancer in 2014. He had spent the latter part of his career specializing in human longevity. And...

item.title: The Download: a battery pivot to AI, and rewriting math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why this battery company is pivoting to AI Qichao Hu doesn’t mince words about the state of the battery...

item.title: The snow gods: How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app
item.description: The best snow-forecasting app for skiers and snowboarders isn’t from any of the federally funded weather services. Nor from any of the big-name brands. It’s an independent app startup that leverages government data, its own AI models, and decades of...

item.title: Are high gas prices good news for EVs? It’s complicated.
item.description: I live in a dense city with plentiful public transportation options and limited parking, so I don’t own a car. I’m often utterly clueless about the current price of gasoline. But as the conflict in Iran has escalated, fossil-fuel prices have been on...
